sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Fund Tx3 by TxPipe as an open API layer for Cardano dApp protocols, including standardized protocol interfaces, documentation, multi-language SDKs, live JSON-RPC endpoints, agent-first documentation, protocol skills, and an MCP server.
Recipient: TxPipe via Intersect treasury withdrawal administration

Deliverables: 12 additional protocol onboardings over 12 months, described as 3 per quarter, with developer and agent coverage, Development of an MCP server for agents to discover and call published protocols, Development of a protocol skills framework and agent-facing documentation, Open API Layer for Cardano Protocols budgeted at 1,635,000 ADA, Intersect Budget Administration fee budgeted at 49,050 ADA

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, while the on-chain withdrawal and budget summary total 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA administration fee.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer claims there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild understanding independently.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Tx3 is described as a framework where each protocol is described once with a verified interface definition that produces documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, and live JSON-RPC endpoints.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposer claims Catalyst Fund 14 has already put the Tx3 developer layer into production with 5 protocols live on the Tx3 Protocol Registry.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal funds 12 additional protocol onboardings, 3 per quarter, plus MCP server and skill framework development as shared infrastructure for all protocols on the registry.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposer claims the proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposer claims there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild understanding independently.

Independent evidence for: TxPipe is stated to have received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals across Funds 9 through 14, with 26 delivered and 4 under development and on schedule, plus 2025 Intersect open-source maintenance funding delivered on schedule.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, while the on-chain withdrawal and budget summary total 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposer claims there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild understanding independently." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
